mnemonics: PUN Threads: Pthreads, User, Native. The three main libraries for threading.
keyPoints: Pthreads is a POSIX standard for C/C++, providing portability across systems.
User-level threads are managed by a thread library at the user level and are faster but lack individual kernel scheduling.
Kernel-level threads (native threads) are managed directly by the operating system, allowing true parallelism on multi-core systems but with more overhead.
commonMistakes: Assuming user-level threads can run in parallel on multiple cores (they cannot, as the kernel sees them as a single process).
Confusing the thread library API (e.g., pthread_create) with the underlying thread implementation (user or kernel).
